dependencies: Dependence on third-party service providers for business operations
Reliance on clearing agents, exchanges, clearing houses, and financial intermediaries for securities and derivative transactions
Dependence on third-party banks for RJBDP (Raymond James Bank Deposit Program)
Reliance on technology systems and data processing systems
risks: Cyber-attacks or security breaches of third-party vendors
Cybersecurity threats, system failures, reliance on third-party technology vendors, operational disruptions
Risk of operational failure, termination or capacity constraints of clearing agents, exchanges, clearing houses or other financial intermediaries
Dependence on third-party service providers with insufficient information security protocols
Technology system failures or inability to effectively update technology systems
Consolidation among clearing agents, exchanges, and clearing houses increasing exposure to certain financial intermediaries

regulatory_risks: OECD Global Anti-Base Erosion Model Rules (Pillar II) requiring minimum 15% effective corporate tax rate in each jurisdiction
FDICIA prompt corrective action requirements
Compliance with Bank Holding Company Act and Federal Reserve capital requirements
FINRA capital requirements and net capital rules for broker-dealer subsidiaries
FDIC insurance requirements and bank regulatory capital standards
Economic sanctions compliance (OFAC, EU sanctions)
DOL fiduciary rule on investment advice
Increased regulatory scrutiny and rulemaking by SEC and other agencies
Extensive regulation and supervision by SEC, Fed, FDIC, OFR, PDBS, CFPB, OCC, NHBD, FINRA, CIRO, FCA and other regulatory bodies
AI-related regulations in various jurisdictions
State and local regulatory requirements across multiple jurisdictions
Basel III capital requirements and potential changes to regulatory capital rules
UK Consumer Duty requirements
Regulation Best Interest and Form CRS compliance
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act compliance
UK Financial Conduct Authority regulations for Charles Stanley operations
SEC Regulation S-P amendments on cybersecurity incident response
Dodd-Frank Act compliance including Volcker Rule restrictions
Fair lending laws (Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Housing Act)
FCA Voluntary Application for Imposition of Requirements (VREQ) restricting onboarding of new branches or financial advisors in UK subsidiary
SEC Division of Enforcement investigation regarding cash sweep programs for investment advisory clients
Anti-money laundering regulations (BSA, PATRIOT Act, AMLA)
Canadian securities regulations for RJ Ltd. operations
Overlapping and divergent regulations across U.S. states and international jurisdictions
Regulatory reviews and inspections by SEC, FINRA, FCA, and other regulatory authorities
SEC settlement for violations of records preservation requirements relating to business communications over unapproved electronic messaging channels - $50 million civil monetary penalty
German regulatory requirements for European operations
State-level regulations on financial services and data privacy
Anti-bribery and corruption laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act)
Community Reinvestment Act obligations for bank subsidiaries
Privacy and data protection regulations (CPRA, GDPR, Canadian privacy laws)
Potential changes to TCJA provisions expiring in 2025
Securities Investors Protection Corporation (SIPC) membership requirements
State-level laws on employment practices and advisor relationships
Basel III regulatory capital reforms implementation
Enhanced prudential standards if assets exceed $100 billion (Category IV bank holding company)
CRA (Community Reinvestment Act) requirements for bank subsidiaries
Resolution planning requirements for insured depository institutions over $50 billion in assets

sanction_risks: Indirect impact of wars and conflicts (Ukraine, Israel) on macroeconomic conditions
Risk of sanctions violations leading to regulatory fines and penalties
Compliance with OFAC economic and trade sanctions programs
